title: Roady v0.11.0: Positioning & Narrative
description:
Roady planning system roadmap:
- v0.5.0 (complete): Horizons 5 & 6 — predictive analytics, domain hardening, event sourcing.
- v0.6.0 (complete): Multi-repo org dashboard, plugin contracts, notification webhooks, fsnotify watch,
  coordinator integration into CLI/MCP with snapshot and task queries.
- v0.7.0 (complete): Org policy inheritance, cross-project drift, plugin registry and health,
  Slack/realtime notifications, watch auto-sync and patterns.
- v0.8.0 (complete): Developer experience polish, API/SDK stability, multi-user collaboration,
  AI-powered workflows, interactive D3 MCP app visualizations.
- v0.9.0 (complete): Expert-review-driven refinements — sharpen onboarding, reduce CLI
  cognitive load, ship a pre-seeded demo, fix empty-state UX.
- v0.10.0 (complete): AI quality and telemetry — eval harness over the planning
  pipeline (heuristic + AI mock + drift corpus), Task.Origin provenance, source-doc
  citations through spec analyze, CompletionResponse confidence + sources +
  streaming OnToken contract, MCP tool consolidation (roady_tasks +
  roady_cost_estimate), naming standardisation aliases, roady notify namespace
  unification, AI command progress feedback with SIGINT cancellation.
- v0.11.0 (complete): Positioning and narrative cleanup — single ICP picked
  (devs using AI coding agents), category named ("Planning memory for AI
  coding agents"), README hero rewritten, feature museum moved to
  docs/advanced.md, docs/vs.md competitive comparison + docs/positioning.md
  one-pager + ROADMAP.md sketch shipped, Astro website hero / definition /
  features sections realigned to match.

features.title: Interactive D3 MCP App Visualizations
features.description: Rich, browser-based D3.js charts embedded in all MCP apps. A shared d3-charts.ts library provides 6 reusable chart functions (donut, force-directed graph, arc gauge, horizontal bars, line chart, collapsible tree) used across 10 apps: status (donut), plan (force DAG), state (donut + swimlanes), drift (severity bars), debt (gauge + donut + bars + line), deps (force network), usage (token gauge), spec (feature tree), policy (compliance gauge), and git-sync (timeline). All charts include hover tooltips, click-to-filter, drag, and zoom interactions.

requirements.title: Add D3 charts to org dashboard app
requirements.description: Add donut chart for project status breakdown and horizontal bars for cross-project progress in org.ts

requirements.title: Refactor forecast app to use shared d3-charts
requirements.description: Replace custom burndown/velocity D3 code in forecast.ts with renderLineChart from d3-charts.ts where applicable

requirements.description: Evaluate if git-sync timeline can use shared library or keep custom (document decision)

requirements.title: Add D3 status visualization to sync app
requirements.description: Add status badge donut or horizontal bars showing sync results in sync.ts

requirements.title: Polish chart interactions across all apps
requirements.description: Ensure consistent hover tooltips, click-to-filter, and zoom behavior across all D3 charts

features.title: Expert Review Refinements (v0.9.0)
features.description:
Cross-functional UX/Product/AI/GTM review identified four high-leverage,
non-breaking refinements to sharpen the new-user activation moment and
reduce CLI cognitive load. Defers breaking changes (MCP tool consolidation,
provider interface refactor, eval harness) to a separate v0.10.0 release.

requirements.title: Group CLI --help output by user intent
requirements.description:
Reorder Cobra command listing in `roady --help` into groups
(Get Started, Track & Report, Integrate, Admin) using cobra
command groups so the golden path surfaces above the noise.
Pure presentation, no API breakage.

requirements.title: Make `roady init` interactive by default
requirements.description:
When `roady init` runs without --template or in a TTY, default
to the existing wizard flow. Add --non-interactive flag for CI.
Prints next-step CTA on completion (run `roady setup <tool>`).

requirements.title: Add `roady demo` command
requirements.description:
New top-level command that scaffolds a pre-seeded .roady/ with
an intentional spec/plan/state divergence and runs drift detect
so a new user sees the product's core value in <60 seconds with
zero prerequisites. Exits cleanly, suggests `roady drift accept`.

requirements.title: Empty-state CTAs on `roady status`
requirements.description:
When `roady status` runs against an uninitialized or empty
project, replace the bare warning with an actionable next-step
ladder: init -> spec analyze -> plan generate -> task ready.
Detects which step is missing and points there.

features.title: AI Quality & Telemetry (v0.10.0)
features.description:
Foundational quality bar for Roady's AI features. Adds a regression
eval harness over the planning pipeline so model swaps and prompt
edits surface measurable behavior changes instead of silent drift.
Non-breaking; complements but does not replace the heuristic plan
generator.

requirements.title: Eval fixture corpus for spec -> plan
requirements.description:
Add an `evals/fixtures/` directory with 2-3 representative spec
inputs (small CLI, web API, multi-feature) and corresponding
golden plan structures (task IDs + dependency edges) used to
anchor regression tests for the heuristic and AI planners.

requirements.title: Eval runner test harness
requirements.description:
Add `evals/runner_test.go` that loads each fixture, invokes the
heuristic PlanService against it, and asserts the produced task
IDs and dependency closure match the golden. Runs under standard
`go test ./evals/...` and is wired into CI naturally.

requirements.title: Eval harness documentation
requirements.description:
Document how to add a new fixture, how to update goldens after
an intentional planner change, and the policy for AI-provider
evals (mock provider in CI, optional matrix run locally).

requirements.title: Task.Origin provenance field
requirements.description:
Add an Origin field on planning.Task ("heuristic" | "ai" | "human")
with a default of "heuristic" so existing data stays valid. The
heuristic planner tags every task it emits as "heuristic"; the AI
planning service tags AI-produced tasks as "ai". Status/plan
readouts surface the origin so reviewers can scrutinise AI tasks.

requirements.title: CompletionResponse confidence and source refs
requirements.description:
Extend ai.CompletionResponse with optional Confidence (0..1) and
SourceRefs ([]SourceRef{Doc, Span}) fields. Provider impls fill
these where supported; callers may ignore. Non-breaking — additive
struct fields only.

requirements.title: Consolidate task-listing MCP tools into roady_tasks
requirements.description:
Add a parameterised roady_tasks MCP tool that supersedes
roady_get_ready_tasks / roady_get_in_progress_tasks /
roady_get_blocked_tasks. Take a status enum parameter
(ready|in_progress|blocked|all). Keep the legacy tools registered
as deprecation aliases that delegate to the new handler so agents
already coded against them keep working.

requirements.title: Pre-flight roady_cost_estimate MCP tool
requirements.description:
Add an MCP tool that estimates input/output tokens and USD cost
for the planned AI operations (generate_plan, smart_decompose,
review_spec, explain_drift, query) before they run. Estimates use
current spec size, the configured AI provider, and a model
pricing table for common providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini,
Ollama). Lets agents and humans decide whether to proceed before
burning tokens.

requirements.title: Standardise MCP tool names with deprecation aliases
requirements.description:
Add canonical aliases for two off-pattern tool names while
keeping the originals registered. roady_smart_decompose gets a
roady_plan_decompose alias. roady_sticky_drift gets a
roady_drift_recurring alias. Both legacy names get a DEPRECATED
marker in their description. Pure additive change.

requirements.title: Unify outbound notifications under roady notify
requirements.description:
Add a top-level roady notify command with add/list/test/remove
subcommands that share the messaging-adapter implementation
today fragmented across roady messaging and roady webhook notif.
Existing commands stay registered as deprecation aliases that
print a one-line migration hint and delegate to the same
handlers. No data migration; both legacy paths keep working.

requirements.title: Eval matrix over AI planner with programmable mock provider
requirements.description:
Extend evals/ to cover the AI planning pipeline (DecomposeSpec)
using a programmable mock provider that returns canned JSON.
Assert tasks parse, the Origin field is set to "ai", required
fields populate, and feature coverage is preserved. Lays the
groundwork for an opt-in real-provider matrix gated on a build
tag so CI never spends tokens.

requirements.title: Drift detector precision and recall corpus
requirements.description:
Add a synthetic-divergence corpus to evals/. Each scenario
stages a spec/plan/state trio with a known mismatch (intent
drift via lock divergence, plan drift via missing requirement,
no drift in the healthy baseline). The runner asserts the
DriftService report contains exactly the expected issue
categories — locking down precision (no false positives on
healthy state) and recall (all known mismatches surface).

requirements.title: Wire source-doc citations through spec analyze to tasks
requirements.description:
Add an optional Source{Doc,Line} on spec.Feature and spec.Requirement
captured during AnalyzeDirectory parsing (markdown file path +
heading line number). Heuristic planner copies the originating
feature's Source onto each emitted task so the plan carries
traceability back to the doc that motivated it. Status output
shows a "from doc:line" annotation when present.

requirements.title: AI command progress feedback and clean cancellation
requirements.description:
Wrap each AI-backed CLI invocation (plan generate --ai, plan
smart-decompose, plan prioritize, drift explain, query) in a
shared helper that prints an elapsed-time tick every few
seconds while the provider is working and translates SIGINT
into a context cancellation so users can abort runaway calls
without leaking the request. Doherty Threshold mitigation;
no provider interface change.

requirements.title: OnToken streaming callback on CompletionRequest
requirements.description:
Add an optional OnToken func(chunk string) field on
ai.CompletionRequest so streaming-capable providers can emit
token chunks as they arrive. Non-streaming providers ignore
the field; existing callers are unaffected (additive only).
Add a streaming-capable mock provider used by tests to lock
the contract. Real per-provider SSE wiring lands separately.

requirements.title: Bridge AI streaming to MCP progress notifications
requirements.description:
Wire an MCP-side helper that, when a ProgressToken is supplied
on the request context, installs an OnToken callback (via
ai.WithOnToken) that forwards each streamed chunk as an MCP
progress notification. Apply to every AI-backed MCP handler:
explain_drift, explain_spec, query, suggest_priorities,
review_spec, smart_decompose. Clients that subscribe to the
progress token see token-by-token output; clients that don't
see no behavioural change.

requirements.title: EVAL_AI build-tag harness for opt-in real-provider matrix
requirements.description:
Add a //go:build evals_ai gated test file that exercises the
real Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini / Ollama providers against a
shared spec when the corresponding API key env vars are set.
Documented in evals/README. Default builds skip it; opt-in
with `go test -tags evals_ai ./evals/...` for local validation.

requirements.title: AI tasks inherit source citations from spec features
requirements.description:
Today the heuristic planner copies spec.Feature.Source / spec.Requirement.Source
onto each emitted task, but tasks produced by the AI planner
(DecomposeSpec) never carry Source because the JSON the model
returns has no source field. Post-process the AI task list and
backfill TaskSource by looking up the originating Feature (and
optionally Requirement) by ID. Same goes for the coverage
fallback path. Result: every task in the plan, regardless of
origin, carries provenance back to the doc that motivated it.

requirements.title: Real provider streaming wired end-to-end
requirements.description:
Replace the contract-only streaming with real implementations:
(a) Anthropic SSE messages endpoint with content_block_delta
parsing, (b) OpenAI Chat Completions SSE delta parsing,
(c) Gemini streamGenerateContent with alt=sse, (d) Ollama
NDJSON Stream:true. Each provider emits via OnToken when set,
falls back to its existing buffered behaviour otherwise, and
populates Confidence from the natural stop signal where one
exists. Wire ai.WithOnToken / OnTokenFromContext through
AIPlanningService so a single CLI/MCP layer setter rides
through all nine AI call sites. CLI withAIProgress installs
the sink, prints streamed tokens to stderr, and steps the
elapsed-time ticker out of the way once tokens flow. Locked
by httptest-backed unit tests for each provider plus an
evals/streaming_runner_test end-to-end check.

features.title: Positioning & Narrative (v0.11.0)
features.description:
Cross-functional GTM consensus: pick a single ICP, name the
category, cut the feature museum from the surface area visible
to first-time visitors. Driven by Dunford / Bush / Lochhead
framings from the v0.10 expert review.

requirements.title: One-page positioning doc
requirements.description:
Add docs/positioning.md following Dunford's "Obviously
Awesome" template: alternative-of, unique attributes, value
those deliver, who cares most, market category.

requirements.title: Rewrite README hero around chosen ICP and category
requirements.description:
Replace the feature-museum README with one sentence + one
workflow. Move advanced features behind a single
"Everything else" link to docs/advanced.md so power users
can still find them.

requirements.title: docs/advanced.md catalogues displaced features
requirements.description:
Receive the bullets cut from README so existing users keep
discoverability. Group by audience: solo dev, small team,
org / enterprise.

requirements.title: docs/vs.md competitive comparison
requirements.description:
Opinionated table comparing Roady to Cursor rules, Claude.md,
spec-kit, Backlog.md, Linear, GitHub Projects. Triangulates
value the Dunford way.

requirements.title: ROADMAP.md sketches the paid wedge
requirements.description:
Public roadmap line for Roady Cloud (hosted MCP, multi-repo
org dashboard, audit retention, SOC2). Signals seriousness
without committing scope; collects waitlist signal later.

requirements.title: Align Astro website hero with new positioning
requirements.description:
Update website/src/pages/index.astro (or equivalent hero
component) so the public site mirrors the README positioning:
single-sentence value prop, single ICP framing, category name
present in hero, trim feature avalanche on the home page.

requirements.title: Reframe IntegrationsSection so it does not contradict ICP
requirements.description:
ICP excludes hand-managed Jira shops yet website front-pages
Linear/Jira "Official Plugins". Either delete from index or
reframe as "Bring your existing tracker along" sub-section so
the AI-native dev does not bounce.

requirements.title: Trim CommandsSection to the actual workflow
requirements.description:
7 groups x ~28 commands defeats the README cleanup two
sections later. Replace with the 5-step workflow snippet
that mirrors README and link out to docs/advanced.md for
the rest.

requirements.title: Refresh MCPSection to canonical v0.10 tool names
requirements.description:
Replace deprecated tool names (roady_smart_decompose,
roady_get_*_tasks) with canonical (roady_plan_decompose,
roady_drift_recurring, roady_tasks). Add roady_cost_estimate.
Fix the AI Interaction example. Collapse the D3 viz sub-block.

requirements.title: Hero primary CTA + Roady Cloud waitlist capture
requirements.description:
Replace the three GitHub-leaking Quick Links with one primary
on-site CTA (anchor to actual-workflow section) and one
secondary email capture for the Roady Cloud waitlist that the
ROADMAP teases. Stop leaking visitors before the value prop
sinks in.

requirements.title: Mark category claim as hypothesis until tested
requirements.description:
Add an explicit "Validated against" line to docs/positioning.md
so the team treats "planning memory for AI coding agents" as a
hypothesis until tested in r/ClaudeAI / r/cursor / MCP Discord
with the ICP. Anti-self-deception guard.

requirements.title: Synthetic ICP interview + internet research findings
requirements.description:
Run web research across r/ClaudeAI, dev.to, Medium, IBM, Wire,
Stack Overflow on (a) phrases the ICP uses for the pain Roady
solves and (b) the competitor ecosystem (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md,
spec-kit, Backlog.md, mcp-memory-service, BMAD). Synthesise a
synthetic interview with a representative ICP persona grounded
in the verbatim language found. Write findings to
docs/positioning.md "Validated against" so future copy is
informed by actual vocabulary.

requirements.title: Cut and publish the v0.10.0 GitHub release
requirements.description:
Tag v0.10.0 from current main, draft release notes summarising
v0.9 + v0.10 features, push tag so the existing GoReleaser CI
workflow builds binaries and attaches them to the release, then
verify the release page renders correctly.

features.title: Pre-launch infra hardening (v0.11.x)
features.description:
Close the gaps surfaced by the "is roady ready for the world?"
audit so the project survives the first wave of public scrutiny
without embarrassing infra failures.

requirements.title: Attach plugin archives to GitHub releases
requirements.description:
Update .goreleaser.yaml so the plugin builds (linear / jira /
github / asana / notion / trello / mock) emit archives that
land on the release page next to the main roady binary.
Plugin users currently hit a dead end on the release page.

requirements.title: Add golangci-lint gate to CI
requirements.description:
Append a golangci-lint step to .github/workflows/ci.yml so
future contributions cannot regress lint silently. Use the
repo's .golangci.yml (v2 config). Fail the workflow on new
issues.

requirements.title: Release smoke-test in CI
requirements.description:
After goreleaser publishes, download the linux/amd64 archive
from the just-cut release, extract, run `roady --version`
and `roady demo`, fail the workflow if either errors. Catches
broken artifacts before users do.

requirements.title: Make Cloud waitlist form honest
requirements.description:
Today the form stores emails in localStorage only — collects
no signal. Replace with a mailto: fallback or a real backend
endpoint, OR remove the form entirely until Cloud has an
alpha date. Pick one; the form-without-backend pattern is
dishonest.

requirements.title: Wire automated website deploy via GitHub Pages
requirements.description:
Today the Astro site builds cleanly but has no deploy
pipeline. Add a Pages workflow that builds website/ on every
main push and publishes to gh-pages so the public site stays
in sync with the README and docs.

requirements.title: Add CHANGELOG.md with goreleaser-driven generation
requirements.description:
Source readers currently have no version diff outside of
the GitHub releases page. Add CHANGELOG.md and configure
goreleaser to write it on each tag.

requirements.title: Round 2 web research + synthetic interviews
requirements.description:
Stress-test the refined positioning ("drift-aware planning
for AI coding agents") with two more synthetic ICP personas
(not just "Sam"): an indie dev shipping multiple side
projects with Cursor and a startup CTO running a small team
on Claude Code. Mine actual phrasing across r/cursor and
HackerNews threads. Update docs/positioning.md.

requirements.title: Document HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN + Pages enablement
requirements.description:
Add a launch-prereqs section to CONTRIBUTING.md or the README
with the exact GitHub repo settings the maintainer must
enable: HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN secret, GitHub Pages
source. Cannot be set via code; needs maintainer action.

requirements.title: Enable gofmt in golangci-lint config
requirements.description:
Add gofmt (or gofumpt) to the enabled linters list in
.golangci.yml so format drift cannot regress past CI.

requirements.title: Wire coverctl coverage gate into CI
requirements.description:
Install coverctl in the build job (go install
github.com/felixgeelhaar/coverctl/cmd/coverctl@latest), run
`coverctl check --from-profile -p coverage.out` after the
existing test step so the coverage thresholds in
.coverctl.yaml actually gate merges.

requirements.title: Remove deprecated verdict step from CI
requirements.description:
verdict is deprecated; the existing step is a no-op anyway
(continue-on-error + binary likely missing on runners).
Delete the step; nox stays a local-pre-commit advisory
because the nox binary is not publicly distributed.

features.title: AI-Powered Workflows
features.description:
AI spec review, auto-prioritization, smart decomposition,
and natural language query interface for project status.

requirements.title: AI spec quality review
requirements.description: AI spec quality review with completeness scoring

requirements.title: AI priority suggestions
requirements.description: AI task priority suggestions from spec and dependency analysis

requirements.title: Context-aware task decomposition
requirements.description: Context-aware decomposition using codebase analysis

requirements.title: Natural language query interface
requirements.description: Natural language query interface for project status

constraints.description: Maintain backward compatibility with existing spec.yaml and plan.json formats
Achieve 80%+ test coverage on new packages
Follow existing DDD patterns and naming conventions
All new CLI commands should have corresponding MCP tools
